searchLedger.hypothesis: Missing provenance columns do not crash persistence

searchActions.result: persistSourceKind returns when the database is absent or memory_index lacks source_kind (write-provenance.ts:119-127). persistProvenanceMetadata builds SET clauses only for existing columns and returns when none exist (write-provenance.ts:130-152). getTableColumns catches PRAGMA failures and returns an empty set (write-provenance.ts:96-104).

searchLedger.rationale: The schema guards correctly degrade to no-op when source_kind/provenance columns are absent.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Column absence is checked before UPDATE statements, and PRAGMA errors are caught.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Genuine manual no-provenance paths still derive human

searchActions.result: deriveSourceKindFromContext returns 'human' when no source/actor/tool/scope/path signal matches (write-provenance.ts:70-93). Direct memory_save only supplies explicit provenance when provenanceSource/provenanceActor are present (memory-save.ts:3519-3528), and human memory_update calls without __provenanceContext use the same default-human path.

searchLedger.rationale: Manual writes remain able to overwrite automated rows as intended; the unsafe case is automated callers that also arrive without provenance context.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No regression found for genuine human/manual writes deriving human.

title: memory_update classifies missing provenance as human before guarding protected fields
evidence: memory_update reads only private args.__provenanceContext (memory-crud-update.ts:74-80) and derives sourceKind only from that object's provenanceSource/provenanceActor/tool (memory-crud-update.ts:98-103). Empty context reaches deriveSourceKindFromContext's final return 'human' (write-provenance.ts:70-93). The public schema exposes only id/title/triggerPhrases/importanceWeight/importanceTier/allowPartialUpdate (tool-input-schemas.ts:300-307), while the mutation proceeds through vectorIndex.updateMemory and persistSourceKind (memory-crud-update.ts:321-325).
rationale: An automated caller using the normal memory_update surface without injected private context is treated as human, so protectExisting is false and protected manual fields can be overwritten while the row remains source_kind='human'.

title: Same-path save/reindex retires manual predecessors before any source_kind guard
evidence: The changed same-path branch computes samePathSupersededPredecessorId then calls retirePredecessorForActiveReindex before inserting the replacement (memory-save.ts:2603-2612), and only applies provenance to the new row afterwards (memory-save.ts:2641-2643). The retire helper checks only constitutional tier and then sets importance_tier='deprecated' (lineage-state.ts:1365-1381), with no source_kind='human' protection. Automated scans do thread import/system provenance into this path (memory-index.ts:900-904; memory-save.ts:2855-2860), but it is not used before mutation.
rationale: An automated scan/import can deprecate a human manual predecessor and replace the active row before the guard has a chance to enforce the constitutional rule. Persisting provenance post-insert is too late for ingress protection.

title: Feedback auto-promotion updates importance_tier without source_kind guard
evidence: AUTO_PROMOTION_PROVENANCE marks this as feedback (auto-promotion.ts:73-77), but checkAutoPromotion reads only importance_tier/validation_count/confidence (auto-promotion.ts:140-142) and executeAutoPromotion directly updates importance_tier (auto-promotion.ts:259-261). It then calls persistProvenanceMetadata only (auto-promotion.ts:262), not persistSourceKind or an ingress guard.
rationale: Feedback automation can change the protected importance_tier of a human/manual normal or important row. The row's source_kind remains human, so the automated change both overwrites manual state and is then shielded as human-authored.

title: PE reinforcement mutates rows without provenance context
evidence: The PE REINFORCE branch calls reinforceExistingMemory (save/pe-orchestration.ts:112-116). reinforceExistingMemory directly updates memory_index stability/importance_weight/content_text/content_hash/review fields (pe-gating.ts:208-218) and has no WriteProvenanceContext or applyWriteProvenance call; the separate updateExistingMemory path does hardcode prediction-error-gate provenance (pe-gating.ts:333-339).
rationale: If the reinforce path is reached, an automated save mutates an existing row without tagging the write, leaving a human row source_kind as human after automated mutation. Adversarial self-check: cross-file REINFORCE is usually downgraded and same-path candidates are normally excluded, so this is narrower than the active P0 paths.
